payload.text:
# Merge Request

## Source Branch
task/skyscribe-lane-1-20260511T062757

## Target Branch
orch/skyscribe-20260511T062757

## Merge Message
merge: wave 1 lane 1 — TP-003

## Tasks Completed
- TP-003: (unknown)

## File Scope
- (no file scope declared)

## Verification Commands

## Result File
result_file: /home/skyscribe/srcs/mini-program/.pi/merge-result-w1-lane1-skyscribe-20260511T062757.json
Write your JSON result to EXACTLY this path (do NOT modify or convert it): /home/skyscribe/srcs/mini-program/.pi/merge-result-w1-lane1-skyscribe-20260511T062757.json

## Result JSON Schema (required)
Use EXACT snake_case keys shown below. Do not use camelCase or shortened keys.

```json
{
  "status": "SUCCESS" | "CONFLICT_RESOLVED" | "CONFLICT_UNRESOLVED" | "BUILD_FAILURE",
  "source_branch": "<source branch name>",
  "target_branch": "<target branch name>",
  "merge_commit": "<merge commit sha or empty string>",
  "conflicts": [{ "file": "...", "type": "...", "resolved": true|false }],
  "verification": { "ran": true|false, "passed": true|false, "output": "..." }
}
```

Do NOT use keys like source/sourceBranch/target/mergeCommit.
Write valid JSON only (no markdown around the final file).

## Important
- You are working in an ISOLATED MERGE WORKTREE (not the user's main repo)
- The correct branch is ALREADY checked out — do NOT checkout any other branch
- Simply merge the source branch into the current HEAD
- Run ALL verification commands after a successful merge
- If verification fails, revert the merge commit before writing the result
- Write the result file LAST, after all git operations are complete

payload.argsPreview: git status && echo "---" && git log --oneline -3 && echo "---" && git branch -v
git log --oneline --graph --all | head -20 && echo "---" && git merge-base HEAD task/skyscribe-lane-1-20260511T062757
git merge-base --is-ancestor task/skyscribe-lane-1-20260511T062757 HEAD && echo "Source is ancestor of HEAD - already merged" || echo "Source is NOT ancestor - need to merge"

cat backend/package.json 2>/dev/null | grep -A 20 '"scripts"' || echo "No backend package.json" && echo "---" && cat admin/package.json | grep -A 20 '"scripts"' && echo "---" && cat miniprogram/package.json | grep -A 20 '"scripts"'
